title: Raccoon Removal in New Brunswick, NJ | $900–$4,000
metaDescription: Professional raccoon removal in New Brunswick, NJ. Safe, humane trapping and exclusion services for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for a free quote!
intro: New Brunswick residents facing raccoon problems need fast, effective solutions to protect their homes and families. Our professional raccoon removal services address infestations in attics, crawl spaces, and around properties throughout the New Brunswick area. With pricing ranging from $900 to $4,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, we provide humane trapping, exclusion, and cleanup services.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over a decade of wildlife management experience to New Brunswick, NJ, with licensed technicians who understand local raccoon behavior patterns and seasonal activity. We offer same-day emergency response, humane removal methods, and comprehensive exclusion work with warranties to prevent future intrusions.
localContext: New Brunswick's mix of historic Victorian homes near downtown, Rutgers University campus buildings, and residential neighborhoods along the Raritan River provides numerous entry points for raccoons seeking shelter. The city's mature trees along Albany Street and Joyce Kilmer Avenue create ideal pathways for raccoons to access roofs and attics, making professional exclusion especially important for local property owners.

faqs.question: How much does raccoon removal cost in New Brunswick, NJ?
faqs.answer: Raccoon removal in New Brunswick typically costs between $900 and $4,000, depending on factors like the number of raccoons, location of the nest, extent of damage, and whether babies are present. A basic single-raccoon removal starts around $900, while complex jobs involving multiple animals, attic cleanup, and extensive exclusion work can reach $4,000.

faqs.question: Are raccoons common in New Brunswick neighborhoods?
faqs.answer: Yes, raccoons are very common throughout New Brunswick, especially in areas near the Raritan River, Buccleuch Park, and residential neighborhoods with mature trees. They're attracted to garbage, pet food, and the many older homes with accessible attics and crawl spaces. Peak activity occurs in spring when mothers seek denning sites and in fall when raccoons prepare for winter.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove raccoons from my New Brunswick home?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day emergency service in New Brunswick for urgent situations. Initial trapping typically begins within 24 hours of your call. Complete removal usually takes 3-7 days depending on the number of animals and whether juveniles are present. We'll assess your specific situation and provide a clear timeline during our initial inspection.

faqs.question: Do you offer cleanup and damage repair after raccoon removal?
faqs.answer: Yes, we provide comprehensive attic cleanup, insulation replacement, and sanitation services to address raccoon droppings, urine, and contaminated materials. We also repair entry points, replace damaged soffits and vents, and install exclusion barriers to prevent re-entry. All cleanup follows New Jersey health and safety guidelines to protect your family from parasites and disease.
